hypothesis: Refine the qualified continuation density branch's revisit depth after the new reversal-balance branch was identified, aiming to keep the same 90+ trade density while improving return quality one more step.

next_priority_queue: treat_swing_threshold_3_with_recovery_15_as_the_least_bad swing baseline rather than a promoted helper
prioritize_the_next_swing_based_branch around score/filter architecture or re-entry rather than longer recovery windows
use_the_swing_structure_replay_as_evidence_that_sweep_to_shift_architecture_is_the_real bottleneck rather than downstream FVG purity alone
treat_context_filter_counters_as_independent diagnostics now that the old elif chain has been removed

deep_backtest.accepted_baseline_reference: Square Blue Termite_trades.csv (not currently present in workspace)

latest_rejected_candidate.reason: The ORB-range trailing stop branch looked viable on Alpaca, but 10-year QC validation rejected it with weaker net profit and higher drawdown than the official v26 baseline.

deep_backtest.next_action: Keep repo QC files on v26-profit-lock. Treat the strict ICT frontier as the benchmark lane at 8 trades / +0.2119% / PF Infinity, preserve the 18-trade active lite frontier as the clean quality reference, use the qualified reversal balance branch at 40 trades / +13.4177% / PF 3.1879 as the middle-density reversal work surface, and keep the newly depth-refined qualified continuation density branch at 93 trades / +11.9309% / PF 1.8204 as the main high-density lane. The next ICT step should either push this 93-trade lane above 100 trades without giving back the stronger return profile or lift the 40-trade reversal branch further toward 50+ trades.

research.analysis_source: The repo continues to treat ORB v26 as the production baseline and ICT as a separate revolutionary development lane. This round adds an ICT-specific walk-forward report for the active lite reversal frontier so the 500-trades roadmap can separate density work from out-of-sample stability evidence.

top_priority.label: ict_lite_reversal_relaxed_smt_looser_sweep_faster_retest_500_trades_branch
top_priority.local_verdict: On the broader paired ICT lane, the strict benchmark remains 8 trades / +0.2119% / PF Infinity and the active lite quality frontier remains 18 trades / +0.4353% / PF 5.0247. The qualified continuation density branch has now been timing-refined to 93 trades / +11.8319% / PF 1.8131 under the standardized capital-based replay, while the nearby 100+ trade branches remain weaker. ICT therefore has a stronger positive higher-density work surface even though it is still not promotion-ready.
top_priority.qc_verdict: No QC evaluator is appropriate yet because the ICT lane is still in paired-data calibration mode and has not formed a promotion-ready trade stack.

ict_position_sizing_compare_phase6h.interpretation: The sizing compare now runs through BacktestEngine itself. Engine-level fixed-share and capital-based sizing materially change the measured return on the active lite frontier, so T04 is complete and sizing can be treated as real infrastructure instead of analyzer-only math.

ict_lite_reversal_baseline_t07.interpretation: The initial lite baseline replay is now standardized to the roadmap's fixed trading spec instead of the old 10-share convention. Density is unchanged, but the economic replay is materially larger, so T07 is complete and should stay off the backlog.

ict_strict_baseline_summary_t01.interpretation: The strict benchmark block is now materialized as a dedicated JSON artifact under the roadmap trading spec, so T01 is complete and later rounds can reuse this file directly.

ict_500_trades_backlog_hygiene_269.interpretation: The 500-trades implementation table contained stale pending rows for work that was already shipped and validated. Removing those rows keeps future rounds focused on live backlog instead of re-reading completed tasks.

ict_promotion_memo_phase8_270.interpretation: The roadmap now has a formal promotion memo. It confirms that the lite ICT branch is the correct research branch, but current density and OOS stability are still too weak to challenge the ORB production baseline.

ict_500_trades_phase_backlog_hygiene_271.interpretation: The top-level 500-trades roadmap still listed phases 0 through 5 as pending even though each phase had already been implemented and written up. Removing those stale rows keeps future table reads focused on truly open work.

ict_500_trades_retest_backlog_hygiene_27.interpretation: The table still mixed live roadmap items with already-shipped lite retest work and analyzer artifacts. Removing those stale rows keeps later rounds from reopening already-promoted retest decisions.

ict_walk_forward_phase7.interpretation: The active lite frontier stays slightly positive on average in holdout, but most folds have no realized trade and only a small minority of holdout windows are positive. Walk-forward is now implemented, but it should be treated as mixed evidence rather than a promotion pass.

ict_rr_gate_phase7b.interpretation: The active lite frontier already clears the repo-approved RR >= 1.5 gate, so the new rule does not change the promoted frontier. Low-target controls confirm the gate is real because it eliminates weak 1.0-RR entries completely.

ict_combined_lanes_phase6g.interpretation: A direct merge of the active lite reversal frontier with the continuation proxy massively increases trade count, but it inherits almost all of the continuation lane's quality collapse. The roadmap should therefore treat combined-lane fusion as completed and rejected.

ict_p0_correctness_repair.interpretation: The old frontier parameter stack still functions after the P0 repair, but the corrected replay is weaker than the pre-repair reference. The first recalibration on top of the corrected logic confirms that fvg_revisit_depth_ratio = 0.5 still improves the corrected structure base, so future calibration should branch from +0.1968% instead of trusting earlier frontier numbers.

ict_short_smt_premium_session_array_ny_o.interpretation: The first post-repair frontier recalibration confirms that the PDF-aligned consequent-encroachment style FVG revisit-depth filter still survives the correctness fixes. The corrected structure base replays at +0.1956%, and 0.50 remains the best robust extension at +0.1968%, so the active frontier keeps FVG_REVISIT_DEPTH_RATIO = 0.5.

After the correctness repair and the post-repair revisit-depth confirmation, the entire tested FVG origin-body axis now collapses to the same frontier result. This makes FVG_ORIGIN_BODY_MIN_PCT a plateaued dimension on the corrected frontier rather than a remaining edge source.

After the correctness repair and the post-repair frontier reset, the FVG origin close-position axis still has coherent geometry, but it no longer creates a stronger edge. Mild gates at 0.60 and 0.70 preserve the exact same frontier, while 0.80+ progressively thins and weakens the lane. This makes FVG_ORIGIN_CLOSE_POSITION_MIN_PCT a survivor-only dimension on the corrected frontier.

After the correctness repair and the post-repair frontier reset, the FVG origin opposite-wick axis still has coherent geometry, but it no longer creates a stronger edge. A mild cap at 0.40 preserves the exact same frontier, while tighter caps progressively thin and weaken the lane. This makes FVG_ORIGIN_OPPOSITE_WICK_MAX_PCT a survivor-only dimension on the corrected frontier.

After the correctness repair and the post-repair frontier reset, the FVG origin range-vs-ATR axis still has coherent geometry, but it no longer creates a stronger edge. Mild gates at 0.50 and 0.75 preserve the exact same frontier, while 1.00+ progressively thin and weaken the lane. This makes FVG_ORIGIN_RANGE_ATR_MULT a survivor-only dimension on the corrected frontier.

After the correctness repair and the post-repair frontier reset, the FVG origin body-vs-ATR axis still has coherent geometry, but it no longer creates a stronger edge. A mild gate at 0.50 preserves the exact same frontier, while 0.75+ progressively thin and weaken the lane. This makes FVG_ORIGIN_BODY_ATR_MULT a survivor-only dimension on the corrected frontier.

After the correctness repair and the post-repair frontier reset, the entire tested FVG origin-lag axis now collapses to the same frontier result. This makes FVG_ORIGIN_MAX_LAG_BARS a plateaued dimension on the corrected frontier rather than a remaining edge source.

After the correctness repair and the post-repair frontier reset, the displacement range-vs-ATR axis still has coherent geometry, but it no longer creates a stronger edge. Mild gates at 0.50 and 0.75 preserve the exact same frontier, while 1.00+ progressively thin and weaken the lane. This makes DISPLACEMENT_RANGE_ATR_MULT a survivor-only dimension on the corrected frontier.

After the correctness repair and the post-repair frontier reset, a light displacement body-quality gate becomes the first upstream extension that still materially improves the lane. Values at 0.10 and 0.20 add one trade and lift the frontier to +0.2119%, while heavier displacement filters quickly thin and weaken it. This makes DISPLACEMENT_BODY_MIN_PCT the new corrected frontier extension.

On top of the corrected paired ICT frontier that already includes the light displacement-body extension, nearby structure-shift close-buffer gates at 0.05 and 0.10 preserve the exact same 8-trade +0.2119% frontier, while 0.15+ progressively weakens the lane. This makes STRUCTURE_SHIFT_CLOSE_BUFFER_RATIO a survivor-only dimension on the stronger corrected helper rather than a new edge source.

On top of the corrected paired ICT frontier that already includes the light displacement-body extension, only a very loose FVG retest touch cap at 5 preserves the exact same 8-trade +0.2119% frontier, while tighter caps progressively thin and weaken the lane. This makes FVG_MAX_RETEST_TOUCHES a survivor-only dimension on the stronger corrected helper rather than a new edge source.

On top of the corrected paired ICT frontier that already includes the light displacement-body extension, replaying the revisit-delay axis confirms that a 3-bar minimum delay still gives the best robust result at 8 trades / +0.2119% / PF Infinity. Shorter 1/2-bar delays and longer 4+-bar delays are weaker, so delayed revisit remains part of the active corrected frontier.

ict_frontier_funnel_phase1.interpretation: The strict frontier is not dying at the last-mile entry trigger. It loses most of its density before arm, especially at premium/discount, SMT, previous-session anchor, and external-liquidity gates, then again at the sweep-to-shift transition. This directly supports the 500-trades roadmap's next step: build a lite ICT reversal baseline that strips the heaviest context blockers while preserving sweep -> structure shift -> displacement -> FVG.

ict_lite_reversal_phase2.interpretation: The first lite reversal branch improves density and return versus the strict frontier, but it is still far from the first 100-trade gate. SMT is now the dominant blocker on the lite lane.

ict_lite_smt_phase2b.interpretation: The lite lane should keep SMT, but in a looser form. Relaxing smt_threshold to 0.0015 is the first robust density extension; fully disabling SMT is only a thinner density control.

ict_lite_geometry_round1_phase3.interpretation: The first clean lite geometry edge is sweep geometry. Loosening liq_sweep_threshold to 0.0006 improves both trade count and total return versus the relaxed-SMT lite frontier, while much looser thresholds become density-only variants.

ict_lite_retest_round2_phase4.interpretation: The first clean retest-aware density edge on the lite lane is slightly faster revisit timing. Reducing fvg_revisit_min_delay_bars from 3 to 2 preserves the 18-trade count and improves total return versus the lite geometry frontier, while removing revisit depth entirely only acts as a thinner density control.

ict_lite_session_density_phase5.interpretation: The first lite session-density pass shows that broader session windows can add one trade, but not without giving back too much return versus the faster-retest frontier. Session broadening is therefore a density control, not the next clean edge.

ict_lite_setup_density_phase5b.interpretation: The first lite setup-density pass shows that longer armed-setup life can add one to two trades, but not without giving back too much return versus the faster-retest frontier. Setup-age expansion is therefore a density control, not the next clean edge.

ict_continuation_compare_phase6.interpretation: The first explicit continuation compare shows that a naive BOS->FVG continuation proxy massively increases density but destroys quality. The continuation proxy should not be promoted and the active lite reversal frontier should remain the repo-approved 500-trades branch.

ict_lite_frontier_funnel_phase6b.interpretation: The active lite frontier is no longer best described as an entry-trigger problem. The main structural collapse now sits between accepted sweeps and shift candidates, while the largest explicit recorded filter is residual SMT. The next 500-trades optimization should target sweep-to-shift conversion and the SMT choke before reopening any new lane.

ict_lite_shift_density_phase6c.interpretation: The first direct sweep-to-shift calibration confirms that shorter structure lookback can materially increase shift candidates and total trades on the lite branch, but not cleanly enough to replace the current faster-retest frontier. Structure_8 should therefore be tracked as a density candidate only.

ict_lite_frontier_smt_recalibration_phas.interpretation: The active-frontier SMT recalibration shows that moderate threshold relaxations can preserve the current lite frontier exactly while reducing raw SMT filter counts, but they do not improve trades or return enough to justify helper promotion. More aggressive relaxation only produces density-only controls, so SMT should no longer be treated as the next clean frontier lever.

ict_lite_frontier_revisit_depth_phase6e.interpretation: The active-frontier revisit-depth recalibration shows that removing the depth gate can add modest density, but only by giving back too much return and profit factor. Revisit-depth should now be treated as completed on the 500-trades backlog rather than a future promotion lever.

ict_lite_frontier_displacement_phase6f.interpretation: The active-frontier displacement recalibration shows that moderate tightening above 0.10 preserves the current promoted branch exactly, while looser displacement gating weakens it slightly. Displacement-body should now be treated as completed on the 500-trades backlog rather than a future promotion lever.

ict_short_smt_premium_session_array_ny_o.interpretation: A PDF-aligned FVG origin directional-body-vs-ATR gate now exists on the displacement candle that creates the FVG, but on top of the stronger delayed-revisit CE frontier it only survives as a nearby continuation. Mild gates at 0.50 and 0.75 stay positive but thinner than the frontier, and 1.00+ weakens the lane progressively. The repo-approved frontier stays on FVG_ORIGIN_BODY_ATR_MULT = 0.0.

A PDF-aligned FVG origin range-vs-ATR gate now exists on the displacement candle that creates the FVG, but on top of the stronger delayed-revisit CE frontier it only survives as a nearby continuation. Mild gates at 0.50 and 0.75 preserve the frontier exactly, while 1.00+ thin or weaken the lane. The repo-approved frontier stays on FVG_ORIGIN_RANGE_ATR_MULT = 0.0.

ict_amd_path_logic.classification_rule: opening session builds an accumulation range, then bullish AMD requires a downside manipulation and midpoint reclaim while bearish AMD requires the mirrored upside manipulation and midpoint rejection

ict_macro_timing_windows.classification_rule: later intraday sweep setups may optionally require the sweep timestamp to fall inside explicit minute windows nested within the broader kill-zone schedule

ict_prev_session_anchor_logic.classification_rule: long setups may optionally require price to still work from the lower half of the previous session range while prior-session buy-side liquidity remains open; short setups use the mirrored condition

ict_session_specific_dealing_array_refin.classification_rule: imbalance windows may optionally require FVG / IFVG delivery arrays while structural windows may optionally require OB / breaker delivery arrays

ict_peer_symbol_data_integration.runtime_behavior: ICT research runs can now merge peer bars from CSV or yfinance, and SMT cannot silently run without real peer data.

ict_peer_smoke_test.interpretation: The real peer-integrated full-stack ICT profile now runs correctly, but produces zero trades on the short local QQQ sample, so the next task is calibration or broader paired-data evaluation rather than more feature layering.

ict_alpaca_paired_profile_calibration.interpretation: On the broader Alpaca paired lane, the full ICT stack still produces zero trades, but a materially relaxed context bundle becomes active and positive. The next step is to calibrate which context filters can be reintroduced without collapsing activity, then preserve the robust survivors as the new calibration base.

ict_paired_activation_frontier.interpretation: On the broader Alpaca QQQ+SPY paired sample, the full ICT stack remains inactive, but a context-relaxed profile becomes active and positive. That enabled a first context-filter reintroduction pass; the next step should now focus on survivor-bundle pairwise calibration instead of adding another conceptual feature.

ict_context_reintroduction.interpretation: The first controlled context-filter reintroduction pass shows that previous-session anchor and external liquidity are robust survivors on the broader paired lane. Premium/discount and session-array refinement remain viable but thinner, while AMD and heavier bundles still collapse activity to zero.

ict_survivor_pairwise_calibration.interpretation: The survivor base remains the top paired-data profile, but the first robust extension that preserves at least half the survivor-base activity is session-array refinement. Premium/discount remains positive but thinner, while daily-bias-heavy stacks still collapse the lane.

ict_survivor_geometry_calibration.interpretation: Once the paired lane is anchored on previous-session anchor, external liquidity, and session-array refinement, a looser sweep threshold at 0.0008 becomes the first robust geometry extension. Tightening the sweep threshold breaks quality, while overly loose SMT confirmation adds activity but degrades profit quality.

ict_survivor_smt_recalibration.interpretation: Once the paired lane is anchored on previous-session anchor, external liquidity, session-array refinement, and a looser sweep threshold, shortening SMT lookback to 10 bars becomes the first robust SMT extension. Disabling SMT entirely expands activity but degrades quality, while looser SMT thresholds add trades without beating the shorter-lookback profile.

ict_short_smt_context_reintroduction.interpretation: Once the paired lane is anchored on previous-session anchor, external liquidity, session-array refinement, a looser sweep threshold, and shorter SMT lookback, premium/discount context can be reintroduced without reducing trades or degrading quality. Daily bias, kill zones, AMD, and heavier bundles still collapse the lane.

ict_short_smt_premium_macro_calibration.interpretation: Macro timing can survive on top of the short-SMT premium base when shifted earlier, but it does not beat the base itself. The premium-enabled profile remains the paired ICT frontier, and macro timing should be treated as a secondary optional branch.

ict_short_smt_premium_daily_bias_calibra.interpretation: Daily bias remains over-restrictive on top of the short-SMT premium base. Every calibrated variant, including shorter-lookback and softer-threshold retests, collapses the lane back to zero trades.

ict_short_smt_premium_kill_zone_calibrat.interpretation: Broader kill-zone windows can survive on top of the short-SMT premium base without reducing trades or degrading quality, but they do not beat the base itself. Default and narrower kill-zone variants still collapse the lane.

ict_short_smt_premium_amd_calibration.interpretation: AMD can be softened enough to remain positive on top of the short-SMT premium base, but only in a thin form that does not preserve enough activity to qualify as robust.

ict_short_smt_premium_external_liquidity.interpretation: Nearby external-liquidity lookback and tolerance tweaks preserve the same frontier result but do not improve it, which indicates a local plateau rather than a new edge.

ict_short_smt_premium_prev_session_ancho.interpretation: Previous-session-anchor on/off and nearby tolerance retests preserve the same frontier result without improvement, which indicates a local plateau rather than a new edge.

ict_short_smt_premium_discount_calibrati.interpretation: Premium-discount lookback and nearby neutral-band retests preserve the same frontier result without improvement, which indicates a local plateau rather than a new edge.

ict_short_smt_premium_session_array_cali.interpretation: Session-array refinement still improves return quality relative to the premium-only control on the current paired ICT frontier, while broader window retests preserve the same best result without beating the default.

ict_short_smt_premium_session_array_macr.interpretation: Macro timing still survives only as a secondary optional branch on top of the short-SMT premium-plus-session-array frontier and remains materially weaker than the frontier itself.

ict_short_smt_premium_session_array_deli.interpretation: All tested delivery-array score and family variants collapse to the same frontier result, which indicates a local plateau rather than a new edge. On the current paired ICT frontier, actual entries are already effectively FVG-only.

ict_short_smt_premium_session_array_ote_.interpretation: All tested OTE geometry and score variants collapse to the same frontier result, which indicates a local plateau rather than a new edge.

ict_short_smt_premium_session_array_fvg_.interpretation: Nearby FVG geometry and freshness retests can survive on the current frontier, but they do not improve it. Shorter-age variants remain positive only by trimming activity and slightly weakening return.

ict_short_smt_premium_session_array_smt_.interpretation: Nearby SMT-threshold retests can survive on the current frontier, but they do not improve it. Looser thresholds preserve the frontier, tighter thresholds weaken it, and turning SMT off raises activity but lowers quality.

ict_short_smt_premium_session_array_kill.interpretation: Nearby kill-zone retests can survive on the current frontier only through broader windows, but they do not improve it. Default and narrower windows still collapse the lane.

ict_short_smt_premium_session_array_sess.interpretation: A NY-only session gate improves the short-SMT premium-plus-session-array base while preserving activity, making it the first robust session extension on that frontier.

ict_short_smt_premium_session_array_ny_o.interpretation: Session-array refinement remains part of the stronger slow-recovery NY-only frontier, but nearby session-array window retests only preserve or weaken the base and do not create a new edge.
The current slow-recovery frontier already clears the relevant confluence gate cleanly, so nearby score-threshold retests only preserve or weaken the base and do not create a new edge.
The current slow-recovery frontier already captures enough close-back strength after the sweep, so nearby reclaim-strength retests only preserve or weaken the base and do not create a new edge.

orb_reentry_profitlock_gated_refinement.interpretation: The structural ORB re-entry branch appears to derive its effect from pre-profit-lock behavior, so profit-lock gating does not rescue the mixed QC result.

orb_hold_ratchet_equivalence.interpretation: The strongest ORB hold ratchet result reproduces the prior structural ORB re-entry branch on the decision metrics that matter, so it should not be treated as a genuinely new post-v26 mechanism.

pullback_reclaim_entry.interpretation: This branch genuinely changed the ORB entry architecture instead of layering another nearby exit patch, and it still failed materially on the longer Alpaca lane.

failed_breakout_reversal.interpretation: This branch genuinely changed the ORB trade structure into a fakeout-reversal design instead of a continuation design, and it still failed materially on the longer Alpaca lane.

opening_regime_classifier.interpretation: This is the first structurally different post-v26 branch to stay positive on Alpaca, but the best variant behaves mostly like delayed continuation instead of a truly balanced continuation / reversal classifier.

orb_trail_qc_rejection.interpretation: The last viable-looking post-v26 branch failed on the 10-year QC data, which closes the remaining nearby mechanism-layering path.
